---
title: "Midterm Group Member Contribution Evaluation"
subtitle: "IBM 6300 — Retailing in the Digital Economy"
author: "Jake Evans"
date: today
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    toc-expand: true
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    code-overflow: wrap
    embed-resources: true
---

## Evaluation Details

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Name | Jake Evans |
| Date | 07/22/2026 |
| Group Name / Project Name | Seis Leches (Group 1) — CPP Farm Store |

## Instructions

Please evaluate your group members (including you) from 1 to 100% scale. If you believe that a member equally contributes to the group project output submitted, give them 100%. If you believe that a member did not do his or her fair share, assign a percentage less than 100%. The evaluation will be confidential. If a person receives grades that are less than 100% from more than one person, the average of peer evaluation will be weighted to group grade to determine an individual grade. Each student must evaluate his/her peers in the absence of the other members to guarantee confidentiality and accuracy.

If there was a group leader who contributed to the group's success greatly above and beyond regular group members, that person may be identified as MVP below and may receive up to 10% of the group's score on top of the group score, if there is a consensus.

## Ratings

| # | Member Name | Grade (%) | MVP (yes/no) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 (self) | Jake Evans | 100% | no |
| 2 | Mickyas Shawel | 100% | **yes** |
| 3 | Tim Arana | 100% | no |
| 4 | Luis Berumen | 100% | no |

## Comments on Teammate Contributions

**Mickyas Shawel** owns and set up our shared GitHub repository and built the GP2 Shopify mini-store prototype (`cpp-farm-store-dev.myshopify.com`), and kept our GitHub Pages deployment running throughout the semester. He repeatedly visited the CPP Farmstore to answer questions for the group project.  His early infrastructure work is what let the rest of the group build and publish deliverables smoothly — **MVP for the group**.

**Tim Arana** contributed to editing and cleaning our data and deliverables across GP1–GP3, helping keep our shared documents and product data accurate.

**Luis Berumen** contributed to editing and cleaning our data and deliverables across GP1–GP3, helping keep our shared documents and product data accurate.

**Jake Evans (self):** Mickey and I led the GP1 field trip report and competitive analysis, and built the GP3 Google Merchant Center product feed (18 offers across 14 products) including the feed-quality diagnosis and product media report. I also contributed to drafting the GP4 campaign plan.

Everyone met their responsibilities this semester and the group worked well together overall.

## Appendix {.appendix}

- **GitHub Repo:** [jakevns/RStudio](https://github.com/jakevns/RStudio/tree/main/IBM6300-PeerEval)
- **GitHub Pages:** [Evans, Jake — IBM 6300 Group Evaluation](https://jakevns.github.io/RStudio/IBM6300-PeerEval/Evans%2CJake-IBM6300-GroupEval.html)
